false, "children": [{"text": "The two primary symptoms of clinical depression is a depressed mood most of the day and a loss of interest in normal activities and relationships, with these symptoms occurring nearly every day for at least 2 weeks. Other symptoms may include: 

 • Fatigue or loss of energy
 • Feelings of worthlessness or guilt
 • Impaired concentration, indecisiveness
 • Insomnia or hypersomnia (excessive sleeping)
 • Markedly diminished interest or pleasure in almost all activities
 • Restlessness or feeling slowed down
 • Recurring thoughts of death or suicide
 • Significant weight loss or gain

People who have depression do not all experience the same symptoms. The severity, frequency, duration, and type of symptoms vary from person to person.", "scrollable-vertical": false, "adapter-view": false, "pointer": "d10e1f8", "scrollable-horizontal": false,
